---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: Error handling and global state
predicate: uses package-level sentinel errors wrapped with %w and forbids global mutable state
object: 'Declare sentinel errors as package-level vars: var ErrNotFound = errors.New("item not found"), ErrAlreadyDone, ErrNothingToUndo, etc. Return them wrapped: fmt.Errorf("task %d: %w", id, ErrNotFound). Callers use errors.Is(). No global mutable var for store or clock — all state lives in the App struct, constructed fresh in main(). Stdlib-only (no third-party deps). Dispatch is a hand-rolled map[string]cmdFunc, not a framework.'
luhmann: "4"
created: "2026-05-30"
source: distilled L2 from build episodes
---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Error handling and global state uses package-level sentinel errors wrapped with %w and forbids global mutable state Declare sentinel errors as package-level vars: var ErrNotFound = errors.New("item not found"), ErrAlreadyDone, ErrNothingToUndo, etc. Return them wrapped: fmt.Errorf("task %d: %w", id, ErrNotFound). Callers use errors.Is(). No global mutable var for store or clock — all state lives in the App struct, constructed fresh in main(). Stdlib-only (no third-party deps). Dispatch is a hand-rolled map[string]cmdFunc, not a framework..
